rounds.name: Round 2: Technical Interview (TR)
rounds.summary: Discussion on REST APIs. What are GET, POST, PUT, DELETE methods? What does status code 404, 500, and 200 mean?

rounds.name: Round 3: HR & Managerial Interview (MR/HR)
rounds.summary: Discussed my leadership qualities and instances where I took initiative in college events.

tips: Prepare NQT mock tests. Verbal section is easy, but quant requires speed. For the interview, revise basic Java/C++ concepts.

rounds.name: Round 1: Online Assessment / Aptitude Test
rounds.summary: Standard NQT test. Numerical questions on averages, percentages, and simple interest. Coding questions: array element search and string duplication removal.

rounds.name: Round 2: Technical Interview (TR)
rounds.summary: Asked about difference between lists and tuples. Explanatory discussion on SDLC (Software Development Life Cycle) model, specifically Agile vs Waterfall.

rounds.name: Round 3: HR & Managerial Interview (MR/HR)
rounds.summary: Asked about my strengths and weaknesses. I explained how I work on my weakness (time management) by using calendar tools.

tips: Practice quantitative and reasoning MCQs. During the interview, show good soft skills and explain project challenges.

rounds.name: Round 1: Online Assessment / Aptitude Test
rounds.summary: Aptitude test with game-based reasoning (inductive, grid challenge, motion challenge) which was very interactive and interesting.

rounds.name: Round 2: Technical Interview (TR)
rounds.summary: Asked to explain Java inheritance, interfaces, abstract classes. Write code demonstrating multiple inheritance using interfaces.

rounds.name: Round 3: HR & Managerial Interview (MR/HR)
rounds.summary: Discussed Capgemini's French heritage and global presence. Asked about my willingness to relocate.

tips: Be strong in Operating System concepts (paging, virtual memory) and explain your coding logic clearly during the interview.

rounds.name: Round 1: Online Assessment / Aptitude Test
rounds.summary: System Engineer online assessment. Had sections on mathematical puzzles, visual reasoning, and pseudocode analysis.

rounds.name: Round 2: Technical Interview (TR)
rounds.summary: Coding question: Find if two strings are anagrams of each other. I wrote an O(n) solution using hashmap.

rounds.name: Round 3: HR & Managerial Interview (MR/HR)
rounds.summary: Asked situational questions: 'If your manager gives you negative feedback, how will you react?'

tips: Communication assessment requires a quiet room and clear pronunciation. For the interview, talk about teamwork and collaboration.

rounds.name: Round 1: Online Assessment / Aptitude Test
rounds.summary: Rapid fire MCQ on cognitive ability and basic IT topics. English section had reading comprehension and error spotting.

rounds.name: Round 2: Technical Interview (TR)
rounds.summary: Discussion on cloud computing. What is SaaS, PaaS, IaaS? What is the difference between SQL and NoSQL databases?

rounds.name: Round 3: HR & Managerial Interview (MR/HR)
rounds.summary: Discussed my hobbies and extra-curricular activities. They asked how I handle pressure and work stress.

tips: Rehearse your project details. Be prepared for SQL queries involving JOINS and aggregate functions. HR round is easy.

rounds.name: Round 1: Online Assessment / Aptitude Test
rounds.summary: Aptitude test containing logical reasoning, quantitative analysis, and verbal skills. Also included a SQL query MCQs section.

rounds.name: Round 2: Technical Interview (TR)
rounds.summary: Asked to write code for finding the 2nd highest salary in a table using subqueries. Also asked about SQL Indexes and views.

rounds.name: Round 3: HR & Managerial Interview (MR/HR)
rounds.summary: Discussed my leadership qualities and instances where I took initiative in college events.

tips: Focus on MS Office, MS Excel shortcuts, and cloud computing definitions. Accenture's cognitive test has strict timing rules.

rounds.name: Round 1: Online Assessment / Aptitude Test
rounds.summary: Cognitive and technical test. Pseudocodes were tricky (bitwise operations and nested loops). English section was focused on prepositions and grammar.

rounds.name: Round 2: Technical Interview (TR)
rounds.summary: Discussion on cloud computing. What is SaaS, PaaS, IaaS? What is the difference between SQL and NoSQL databases?

rounds.name: Round 3: HR & Managerial Interview (MR/HR)
rounds.summary: Asked why I applied for Accenture. I discussed Accenture's innovation centers and their focus on cloud and digital transformation.

tips: Practice solving pseudocodes on platforms like GeeksforGeeks. Accenture's cognitive test has strict timing.

rounds.name: Cognitive and Technical Assessment
rounds.summary: Rapid fire MCQ on cognitive ability, pseudocode debugging, MS Office suite basics, and cloud concepts.

rounds.name: Coding Assessment
rounds.summary: 2 coding questions. One was on array manipulation, another on bitwise operators. Clearing 1 test case gets you through.

rounds.name: Technical & HR Interview
rounds.summary: Discussion about project structure, Git commands used, team conflicts resolved, and goals for the next 2 years.

tips: Focus on verbal and aptitude speed during NQT. For the interview, show confidence and have basic knowledge of DBMS and SDLC.

rounds.name: TCS NQT
rounds.summary: Numerical ability, verbal reasoning, and 2 coding questions (Matrix manipulation and string reversal).

rounds.name: TR/MR/HR Combined Interview
rounds.summary: Single panel interview. Asked about my favorite programming language (Python), explanation of lists vs tuples, basic database normalization, and situational questions like 'how do you handle conflicts in a team?'

tips: Revise OOPs concepts thoroughly and practice basic SQL queries. Knowing your project inside out is critical.

rounds.name: Online Assessment
rounds.summary: Aptitude, logical reasoning, and 2 basic coding questions (Array-based and String-based).

rounds.name: Technical Interview
rounds.summary: Questions on Java (Inheritance vs Polymorphism, Abstract classes, Interface, memory management), SQL queries (Joins, Group By, 2nd highest salary), and my final year project details.

rounds.name: HR Interview
rounds.summary: Basic behavioral questions: relocation willingness, night shift comfort, and background verification overview.
